pr.title: Remove incbins from rodata and fixup pointers
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Replaced many raw .incbin ranges in asm/rodata.s and asm/data.s with explicit assembler data declarations, including numeric .4byte payloads, strings with labels, NULL/zero regions, and pointer tables. The PR also fixed pointer-like entries by expressing them as symbols where evidence was available, such as rodata entries pointing at lbl_803D9DF4 and lbl_803D9E08 and data tables containing labeled string addresses. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the slice, so intent is inferred mainly from the title and diff.

key_files.role: Main rodata de-incbin work; many baserom .incbin blocks were expanded into explicit .4byte data, floats encoded as hex words, zero-filled records, small byte-packed tables, and pointer references.
Data-section fixups; exposed string labels around ToyDsp/Ty*.dat assets and replaced some incbins with explicit values and pointer table entries.

what_changed: Removed many .incbin directives from asm/rodata.s and replaced them with explicit .4byte data matching the original bytes.
Removed several .incbin directives from asm/data.s, notably around lbl_803D9DAC, lbl_803FFFA8, and lbl_80400010.

Added global labels for individual strings inside a previously less-structured string region, including ToyDspMychara*, ToyDspMap*, ToyDspFgseries*, ToyDspFgetc*, ToyDspPokemon*, ToyDspItem*, and Ty*.dat strings.
Converted at least one rodata structure to use symbolic pointer operands instead of raw addresses, for example lbl_803B7CA8 now contains .4byte lbl_803D9DF4 and .4byte lbl_803D9E08.
Expanded packed table data as hex .4byte values, preserving byte layout for arrays that were not yet typed as C structs or narrower element declarations.
Kept alignment behavior around strings with .balign 4 while adding labels at string boundaries.
smart_moves: Used labels for string boundaries before fixing up pointer tables, making future references searchable and relocatable instead of relying on magic addresses.
Preserved exact data layout with conservative .4byte hex values where semantic types were uncertain, avoiding speculative C typing while still eliminating incbins.
Replaced raw pointer-looking data with symbolic labels where possible, which is safer for relocation and for later migration into C data definitions.
Maintained .balign 4 after asciz strings, which is important because adding labels inside a string table can otherwise accidentally change following addresses.
Broke large anonymous incbin blobs into smaller labeled records, improving cross-reference discovery for later decompilation.
decomp_lessons: A productive early data cleanup step is to de-incbin rodata/data into exact assembler declarations before attempting to name or type every field.
When bytes represent addresses, prefer symbolic .4byte label references over absolute address constants whenever a target label is known.
For packed arrays or unknown structs, .4byte hex preserves the original byte ordering and avoids premature assumptions about signedness, element width, or struct layout.
String-table decompilation should add labels at each referenced string, not just at the start of the contiguous string region, so later pointer arrays and C declarations can refer to stable symbols.
Keep explicit alignment directives after strings when splitting a blob into labels; alignment is part of the binary match contract.
Large incbin removal PRs can be valuable even without semantic names because they expose hidden pointers and constants to the assembler/linker.
naming_conventions: This PR retained address-derived labels such as lbl_803B7CA8, lbl_803D9DF4, lbl_803FF604, and lbl_80400010 rather than introducing speculative semantic names.
Jump-table naming such as jtbl_803D9E9C was preserved where already present.
New string labels follow the existing global-address label convention, using lbl_<address> at the exact start of each string.
Asset string contents preserve original capitalization and spelling, such as ToyDspMycharaCmA_Top_matanim_joint, TyMycCmA.dat, TyMapA.dat, TyPokeA.dat, and TyItemA.dat.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace `.incbin "baserom.dol", offset, size` with sequences of `.4byte` values whose total byte count matches the removed blob.
Represent null/zero-filled data as repeated `.4byte 0x00000000` rather than leaving it inside incbins.
Use `.asciz` plus `.balign 4` for embedded strings and add `.global` labels before strings that may be pointed to individually.
Convert known pointer entries from raw numeric addresses to symbolic label references, as shown by `.4byte lbl_803D9DF4` and `.4byte lbl_803D9E08`.
Keep unknown floats as their exact IEEE-754 hex word values when the diff does not establish semantic names or typed declarations.

For pointer tables still represented as absolute words, such as the lbl_803FFFA8 table containing 0x803FFDA0 through 0x803FFF94-like addresses, the evidence shows a de-incbin step but not necessarily a full symbolic conversion in the excerpt.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided slice.
Because there was no reviewer discussion, no explicit reviewer intent or requested follow-up can be extracted.
searchable_terms: Remove incbins from rodata and fixup pointers
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert lbl_803B7CA8 or nearby rodata records into typed C structs.
Search for references to ToyDspMycharaCmA_Top_matanim_joint and TyMycCmA.dat to identify the owning subsystem.
Check whether the absolute pointer table at lbl_803FFFA8 was later converted from numeric addresses to symbolic labels.
Search later history for remaining .incbin directives in asm/rodata.s around lbl_803B75xx through lbl_803B7Exx.
Look for decompilation work that names lbl_803D9DF4, lbl_803D9E08, or jtbl_803D9E9C.
